goal: Create a no-code decision map that sorts post-BPK-226 Bluepilot surfaces against the 10 soulmatch hard caps before any trim, activation, write, or deletion work.
scope_out: Do not delete files, trim code, add or change runtime gates, add routes, change provider/write/runtime behavior, change environment variables, change DB/schema behavior, deploy, merge, or activate any surface.
claims: Provider budget/cost gating exists through assertBuilderBudgetGate, assessBudget, recordBuilderCost, and recordCost.
permitApply.ts is a real write/push endpoint and should be retained, but its branch hard cap for main/master needs a later small fix.
activationLockBoundary.ts and runtimeExecutionDecision.ts are useful central execution boundaries.
activationDecisionOperatorMode.ts and mayaAutonomyAuthorityIntake.ts use an older generic hard-stop list that should be aligned to the 10 soulmatch hard caps.
The runtimePatchPermitConsume receipt/audit chain is a trim candidate, but only after a dependency graph and test impact map.
assumptions: BPK-229 is stacked on BPK-228 so the soulmatch autonomy reference is present in the build prompt.
A decision map is the correct next step before code deletion or safety-gap fixes.
dod: docs/POST-BPK-226-TRIM-MAP.md lists the 10 hard caps and sorts the post-226 surfaces into keep, align/fold, trim candidates, and build-later gaps.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md record the BPK-229 map and safety boundary.
docs/CLAUDE-CONTEXT.md references BPK-229 in the next-block context.
No code, tests, package files, runtime routes, provider behavior, write behavior, env, DB, deploy, or activation changes are made.
required_commands: rg -n "Post-BPK-226 Trim Map|10 Hard Caps|Main/master write cap|local emergency stop|runtimePatchPermitConsume|assertBuilderBudgetGate|permitApply" docs/POST-BPK-226-TRIM-MAP.md STATE.md docs/SESSION-LOG.md docs/CLAUDE-CONTEXT.md
stop_conditions: Any code deletion, runtime/provider/write/route/test change, env/DB/deploy activation, or merge becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-228 anchored the existing soulmatch autonomy family in the Bluepilot build prompt. Follow-up code reading found that provider budget/cost gating exists, permitApply is a real write endpoint, the old hard-stop lists do not match the 10 soulmatch caps, and the runtimePatchPermitConsume receipt/audit tower is the strongest trim candidate.
